summary: A lively, Bob‑tune heavy show captured on a soft‑mellow soundboard that’s pleasant despite modest guitar presence.
review: The concert is praised for its fun vibe and strong Bob Weir songs even though Jerry was battling laryngitis; reviewers note the short, all‑Bob set as “smokin’”. The SBD recording is described as soft and mellow with clear vocals, though some wish Jerry’s guitar were more forward in the mix.

ai_rating.rationale: Three detailed reviews give a consistent picture of an enjoyable performance and a decent soundboard recording; minor mix concerns keep the rating just below top‑tier.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Soft, mellow board mix with vocals up front; guitar slightly recessed but overall clear and listenable.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Short set consisting only of Bob Weir tunes; energetic and fun despite Jerry’s limited vocal contribution.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Illness kept him from singing much and his guitar sits a bit back in the mix, but his playing still adds to the show.
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted for his enthusiastic pre‑encore comment and his “10‑button” bass work.
band_member_comments.Bob: His voice is front‑center and praised as being in good shape; the set focuses on his songs.
